mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, or forward 60-day returns are negative in > 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during an economic expansion reflects robust manufacturing output, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of expansion regime observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 3.0%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation observations, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across the economy, which reliably predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while the unemployment rate rises during a late-cycle phase signals an impending recession, which rapidly deteriorates earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of divergence observations, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward falsification.
